title: Hourly to Annual Salary Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate your annual salary from an hourly wage. Enter your rate, hours/week, and weeks worked to get annual, monthly, biweekly, weekly, and daily pay.
intro: Not sure what your hourly wage translates to as an annual salary? This calculator converts any hourly rate into **annual, monthly, biweekly, weekly, and daily** gross figures. Adjust hours per week and weeks worked per year to reflect your actual schedule — including unpaid time off. Overtime at **1.5×** over 40 hours/week is calculated automatically.
keyTakeaway: At $20/hr working 40 hours/week for 52 weeks, your gross annual salary is $41,600 — or $3,466.67/month before taxes.
useCases: Comparing a job offer's hourly rate to your current salaried position
Estimating annual income for a loan or rental application
Calculating how a part-time schedule affects your yearly earnings
Figuring out total annual pay when you regularly work overtime
Budgeting for a year when you plan to take unpaid weeks off
Converting a contractor's hourly rate to an equivalent full-time salary

options.label: No overtime — flat rate for all hours
Overtime at 1.5× for hours over 40/week (US standard)

explanation:
## What is hourly to annual salary conversion?

Hourly to annual salary conversion multiplies your hourly rate by total hours worked yearly. At $20/hour working 40 hours weekly for 52 weeks, annual salary equals $41,600. This calculation accounts for variations in weekly hours and paid weeks to determine gross income before taxes and deductions.

## How It Works

This calculator converts an hourly wage into equivalent salary figures across common pay periods.

## Core Formula

```
Annual (no OT) = hourly_rate × hours_per_week × weeks_per_year

Annual (with OT, hours > 40) =
  (regular_hours × hourly_rate + overtime_hours × hourly_rate × 1.5)
  × weeks_per_year

Where:
  regular_hours  = min(hours_per_week, 40)
  overtime_hours = max(hours_per_week - 40, 0)

Monthly   = annual ÷ 12
Biweekly  = annual ÷ 26
Weekly    = annual ÷ 52  (or × weeks_per_year ÷ 52 adjusted)
Daily     = weekly ÷ 5
Effective hourly = annual ÷ (hours_per_week × weeks_per_year)
```

## Worked Example

Assume: $18/hr, 45 hours/week, 50 weeks/year, with 1.5× overtime.

- Regular hours per week: 40 → $18 × 40 = **$720**
- Overtime hours per week: 5 → $18 × 1.5 × 5 = **$135**
- Weekly gross: $720 + $135 = **$855**
- Annual: $855 × 50 = **$42,750**
- Monthly: $42,750 ÷ 12 = **$3,562.50**
- Biweekly: $42,750 ÷ 26 = **$1,644.23**
- Daily: ($855 ÷ 5) = **$171.00**
- Effective hourly: $42,750 ÷ (45 × 50) = **$19.00/hr**

## Pay Period Reference Table

| Period | Divisor / Multiplier |
|---|---|
| Annual | base |
| Monthly | ÷ 12 |
| Semi-monthly | ÷ 24 |
| Biweekly | ÷ 26 |
| Weekly | weeks_per_year used |
| Daily | weekly ÷ 5 |

## Limitations & When NOT to Rely on This Alone

- **Gross only.** Federal income tax, FICA (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%), state and local taxes are NOT deducted. Your take-home pay will be lower.
- **No benefits valuation.** Employer-provided health insurance, 401(k) match, PTO, and other benefits can add 20–40% on top of gross wages — this calculator does not include them.
- **Overtime eligibility varies.** The 1.5× overtime rate reflects the US Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) threshold of 40 hours/week for non-exempt employees. Exempt salaried workers, freelancers, and workers in other countries may have different rules.
- **Weekly hours consistency.** If your hours vary week-to-week, use your average. The result is an estimate of your annualized earnings, not a guarantee.

faq.q: What is the standard formula for hourly to annual salary?
faq.a: The standard formula is: Annual = hourly rate × hours per week × 52. For a 40-hour workweek at $25/hr, that's $25 × 40 × 52 = $52,000/year. Adjust weeks worked if you take unpaid leave.

faq.q: How does overtime affect my annual salary calculation?
faq.a: Under the US FLSA, non-exempt employees earn 1.5× their regular rate for hours over 40 in a workweek. If you work 45 hours at $20/hr, your weekly pay is (40 × $20) + (5 × $30) = $950, not $900.

faq.q: Should I use 52 weeks or account for vacation?
faq.a: Use 52 if you are paid for all weeks (salary or paid PTO). Reduce weeks worked if you take unpaid time off. For example, 2 weeks of unpaid leave = 50 weeks. This lowers your annual total proportionally.

faq.q: What is the difference between gross and net (take-home) pay?
faq.a: Gross pay is your total earnings before deductions. Net pay (take-home) is after federal/state income tax, FICA taxes (7.65% employee share), health insurance premiums, and retirement contributions. This calculator shows gross only.

faq.q: How is biweekly pay calculated from an annual salary?
faq.a: Divide your annual gross by 26 (there are 26 biweekly pay periods in a year). Example: $52,000 ÷ 26 = $2,000 per biweekly paycheck. Note: some months have 3 biweekly paydays.

faq.q: Is $15/hr a good annual salary in the US?
faq.a: $15/hr at 40 hours/week, 52 weeks = $31,200/year gross. The 2025 federal poverty guideline for a single person is $15,650/year, so $31,200 is above that threshold, but purchasing power varies significantly by location.

faq.q: How do I calculate my daily rate from an hourly wage?
faq.a: Multiply your hourly rate by the number of hours in your workday. For an 8-hour day at $20/hr, daily rate = $160. This calculator assumes a 5-day workweek (weekly ÷ 5) for the daily output.

faq.q: What does 'effective hourly rate' mean in the results?
faq.a: The effective hourly rate is your total annual earnings divided by total hours worked (hours/week × weeks/year). When overtime is included, this figure exceeds your base rate because overtime hours are paid at 1.5×.

faq.q: Does this calculator apply outside the US?
faq.a: Yes — the core math applies globally. However, overtime rules, statutory leave entitlements, and tax rates differ by country. Enter your local hourly rate and actual hours/weeks. Tax deductions are not calculated regardless of country.

faq.q: How many working hours are in a standard US year?
faq.a: A standard full-time US work year is 2,080 hours (40 hours/week × 52 weeks). Accounting for 10 federal holidays, it drops to approximately 2,000 paid hours, though this varies by employer policy.

dataUpdate.notes: Review FLSA overtime thresholds, FICA rates, and federal poverty guidelines annually. Core arithmetic formula is stable.
